content.text:
You are applying code migration fixes to a single file.

File: /home/jmontleo/semver-analyzer/build/batch-run-20260604T185102/odf-console/source/packages/odf/components/mcg/log-replication-info-form.tsx

Apply ALL of the following 2 fixes to this file:

### Fix 1
Line: 59
Rule [sd-icon-children-to-prop-helpertextitem]:
In PatternFly v6, icon content in <HelperTextItem> must be passed to the `icon` prop instead of as children.

If <HelperTextItem> contains only an icon element (from @patternfly/react-icons), move it to the `icon` prop and make the element self-closing:
  Before: <HelperTextItem variant="plain"><TimesIcon /></HelperTextItem>
  After:  <HelperTextItem variant="plain" icon={<TimesIcon />} />

If <HelperTextItem> contains an icon AND text, move just the icon:
  Before: <HelperTextItem><PlusCircleIcon /> Add item</HelperTextItem>
  After:  <HelperTextItem icon={<PlusCircleIcon />}>Add item</HelperTextItem>

Incident context:
  componentName: HelperTextItem
  module: @patternfly/react-core
  parentFrom: @patternfly/react-core
  parentName: HelperText

Fix strategy:
Strategy: LlmAssisted
Component: HelperTextItem
Prop: icon

Code context:
```
   58              <HelperText>
   59                <HelperTextItem>
   60                  {t(

```

### Fix 2
Lines: 29, 44
Rule [sd-new-absorbing-prop-stackitem-component]:
Component <StackItem> has a new prop `component` (type: `React.ReactNode`) added in v6 that accepts JSX content.

This prop may be intended to receive content that was previously passed as children. Review the JSX children of <StackItem> and determine if any should be moved to the `component` prop.

Example migration:
  Before: <StackItem>{content} other children</StackItem>
  After:  <StackItem component=>other children</StackItem>

Incident context:
  componentName: StackItem
  module: @patternfly/react-core
  parentFrom: @patternfly/react-core
  parentName: Stack

Fix strategy:
Strategy: LlmAssisted
Component: StackItem
Prop: component
To: React.ReactNode

This rule affects multiple locations in the file. Apply ALL steps together as one logical change.

Code contexts:
```
  (line 29):
   28      <Stack hasGutter className="odf-mcg__form">
   29        <StackItem>
   30          <FormGroup fieldId="logs-location" label={t('Event log bucket')}>

  (line 44):
   43        </StackItem>
   44        <StackItem>
   45          <FormGroup fieldId="log-prefix-input-field" label={t('Prefix')}>

```

Instructions:
1. Read the file at /home/jmontleo/semver-analyzer/build/batch-run-20260604T185102/odf-console/source/packages/odf/components/mcg/log-replication-info-form.tsx
2. Process each fix INDEPENDENTLY in sequence. For each fix:
   a. Identify the exact code affected (line number and affected element)
   b. Determine the specific change needed (add/remove/rename identifiers, update references)
   c. Track all changes for the final write
3. Make the minimum edits necessary — do not change unrelated code, but DO clean up any artifacts caused by your changes (e.g., remove imports that are no longer referenced, delete dead declarations)
4. After determining your changes, reason through any functional side effects: verify that your edits preserve the existing behavior of the surrounding code. If a migration change makes existing variables, state, or logic redundant, clean up the affected code so the file remains correct and consistent.
5. Do NOT revert any changes that were already applied in previous passes
6. Write the fixed file once with ALL changes from every fix applied
7. REQUIRED: If companion test files are listed above, read EACH one now. Do NOT skip this step. For each test file, check whether your changes to the main file would cause test failures (e.g., portal rendering means getByText/getByRole won't find dropdown content, renamed attributes break selectors). If a test would break, apply the fix and write the updated test file.


Before writing, reason through each fix step by step to ensure nothing is missed. Then read the file, make the edits, and write it.

After writing the file, produce a '## Changes Applied' section that lists each change you made, each fix that was already applied (no change needed), and each fix you could not apply (with a brief reason). This summary is used by subsequent processing steps.
I'll start by reading the file to understand its current state.

Now let me reason through each fix:

### Fix 1 Analysis (Line 59 - HelperTextItem icon prop)
Looking at lines 58-63, the `<HelperTextItem>` contains only text content (a translation string). There is **no icon** inside this `<HelperTextItem>`. The rule says to move icon content to the `icon` prop, but there's no icon here. The rule matched this component but there's nothing to migrate — the `<HelperTextItem>` only has text children. **No change needed.**

### Fix 2 Analysis (Lines 29, 44 - StackItem component prop)
The new `component` prop on `<StackItem>` is of type `React.ReactNode` — but looking at the PatternFly v6 API, `component` on `StackItem` is actually an HTML element type prop (like `"div"`, `"span"`, etc.) that controls what HTML element the StackItem renders as. It's **not** meant to absorb children content. The `<StackItem>` elements at lines 29 and 44 each contain a `<FormGroup>` as children, which is correct usage — the children should remain as children. **No change needed.**

Both fixes describe patterns that don't actually apply to the code in this file. The file is already correct as-is. Let me verify there are no companion test files mentioned:

No test files were listed, so no test changes needed either. The file requires no modifications.

## Changes Applied

| Fix | Rule | Status | Details |
|-----|------|--------|---------|
| Fix 1 | `sd-icon-children-to-prop-helpertextitem` (line 59) | **No change needed** | The `<HelperTextItem>` at line 59 contains only text children (a translation string), not an icon element. The rule applies only when an icon component is present as a child — there is none here. |
| Fix 2 | `sd-new-absorbing-prop-stackitem-component` (lines 29, 44) | **No change needed** | The `component` prop on `StackItem` controls the rendered HTML element type (e.g., `"div"`, `"section"`), not content absorption. The existing children (`<FormGroup>` elements) are correctly placed as children and should not be moved to `component`. |
